The West Portal of Saint-Gilles-du-Gard at the Carnegie Museum of Art is unique because it is one of a kind and arguably the largest architectural cast ever made. It was brought to the museum on the recommendation of art experts, including Andrew Carnegie, and is considered the most beautiful of all the great Romanesque portals and the culminating example of the Provencal Romanesque in southern France. The portal was constructed during more than a century of craftsmanship in the 12th century and is noted for having an early sculptural treatment of the Passion of Christ. The story of Saint Gilles, the hermit priest for whom the portal is named, is also significant, as he was a missionary who lived in the forest near the Gard River and was known for his humility and holiness. The portal served as a remarkable setting for public activities and has inspired generations of Pittsburghers, especially young artists.

The enhancements in the 7.5 release of SAP BusinessObjects Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver, include:

1. Business process flows (BPFs): These provide controllable, consistent, and collaborative planning and consolidation processes. They enable the finance organization to implement processes across the enterprise by designing the logical flow, reviews, and approvals needed to drive planning and forecasting exercises. Users can now have two different BPF views: the Monitor view, which allows executives and reviewers to see the progress of a process (or processes) across the entire organization, and the My Activities view, which provides a personalized view of step completion status and activities assigned to a specific user.

2. Drill-through to source systems: This capability enables users to access data in various targets, including SAP ERP, SAP BW, and SAP CRM, without having to replicate data in the SAP BusinessObjects Planning and Consolidation application. This allows users to explore detailed information on transactions directly from the source systems.

3. Integration with SAP BusinessObjects BI tools: SAP BusinessObjects BI solutions, such as Xcelsius, SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence, and SAP BusinessObjects Explorer, can now be integrated with SAP BusinessObjects Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver. This integration allows companies to leverage these BI solutions to access information directly from SAP BusinessObjects Planning and Consolidation, enabling information to be shared with users beyond the realm of the finance group over the Web. This integration also supports single sign-on capabilities and multiple authentication types via the Central Management Server.

These enhancements aim to improve agility, reduce cycle time, and increase user productivity by expanding the reach of SAP BusinessObjects Planning and Consolidation and providing better integration with other SAP solutions.

To increase the likelihood of online giving through a church's website, follow these steps:

1. Evaluate the first impression your church website leaves. Ensure the website is visually appealing, trustworthy, and up-to-date. You can get a free church website evaluation to grade your site based on industry standards.

2. Compare visiting your website to visiting your church. Make sure the website reflects your church's unique personality and provides a consistent experience for visitors, including photos and videos of your church.

3. Make it easy to give. Include a giving button or form on your home page, in your site's navigation, and on every page where it makes sense. Ensure the giving form is easy to use and doesn't require account creation.

4. Create confidence for givers. Make sure your website and giving form are secure, with clean modern design and readable text. The look of your giving form should match the look of your website to create confidence.

5. Don't stop at how. Provide the why by showcasing your church's mission through photos, videos, and stories. Make it about more than just dollars and cents.

By following these steps, you can turn your church website and online giving software into a power duo that works for you and increases the likelihood of online giving.

According to Justin Wasserman, the leadership principles that make Santa Claus successful are:

1. Inspire a Volunteer Army: Santa can't do it alone, and he relies on millions of volunteers (parents and relatives) to help him deliver gifts. Effective leaders develop a vision that appeals to both the logic and emotions of their people, which ignites the diverse motivations of their constituencies and develops sufficient critical mass willing to donate their discretionary time to accomplish the incredible.

2. Make it Fun: Santa understands that increased involvement leads to increased commitment. He leaves the HOW of gift delivery up to every parent and relative around the world, allowing them to have fun and be creative in their approach. Leaders should not over-script the HOW, as this can reduce creativity and imagination among their staff.

3. Embrace Your "Rudolph": Santa's reindeer Rudolph, with his glowing red nose, serves as an aligning beacon during inclement weather, just as a clearly articulated vision must do when faced with growing uncertainty and a rate of change. Visions should not be left to sit on a shelf, but should be communicated effectively to the entire team.

Some of the challenges and considerations when processing high-temperature polymers include:

1. Equipment suitability: Older extrusion lines may not be suitable for processing high-temperature polymers due to their limitations.

2. Heating elements: Heating elements need to provide long-term serviceability at elevated temperatures. Ceramic-insulated heaters are typically substituted for standard mica-insulated heaters on all downstream equipment. Air-cooled heaters are generally used in extruding high-temperature polymers.

3. Barrel expansion: Cast-aluminum barrel heaters can still be used, but the barrel diameter may expand at elevated service temperatures.

4. Watt-density: Heaters require higher watt-density than conventionally equipped extruders to reduce startup times.

5. Degradation: The time at elevated temperature causes more degradation than the temperature level. In some cases, it's necessary to heat even the normally cooled feed throat to obtain a stable feed rate.

6. Drying: High-temperature polymers require drying at relatively high temperatures. High-capacity hopper dryers are often required to maintain the polymer temperature.

7. Energy consumption: Insulation becomes more critical as processing temperatures increase because heat losses to the environment through convection are directly proportional to the difference between the machine and ambient temperatures.

8. Safety: All surfaces of the extruder line are much hotter than usual, requiring better guarding to prevent inadvertent skin contact. Protective clothing may be required, and any cooling system that contains hot oil or steam must be enclosed to prevent accidental exposure.

9. Cooling procedures: After exiting the die, high-temperature polymers usually require special cooling procedures. Cooling with oil is often used to avoid the difficulties of vaporizing water, which may require redesign of the cooling system. Air cooling is often used, but it is not as effective as liquid cooling, and additional cooling length may be required.

10. Equipment arrangement and floor space: The use of air cooling instead of water cooling may change the equipment arrangement and increase the floor space requirement.

Blockchain technology can revolutionize financial markets in several ways:

1. Faster and Lower-Cost Payments: By offering a decentralized database of digital assets, blockchain can facilitate faster payments and lower the fees associated with processing them. This could lead to a new level of service and allow banks to compete with fintech startups.

2. Decentralized Asset Exchange: Blockchain can create a decentralized database of digital assets, which can represent real-world assets such as stocks, commodities, or debts. This would cut out the middleman, lower asset exchange fees, and accelerate the process significantly.

3. Fundraising: Blockchain could simplify the process of fundraising, particularly through Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs), by reducing the complexity and length of negotiations involved in traditional venture capital.

4. Peer-to-Peer Loans: Blockchain could lead to faster and more secure loan processes, as well as the development of complex programmed loans that approximate syndicated loan structures or mortgages.

5. Secure Credit Information: Blockchain could provide a more secure and efficient way of processing loan applications by storing sensitive credit information on a decentralized, secure, and error-free system. This would reduce the risk of data breaches and protect consumers' sensitive information.

The main difference between clean eating and flexible dieting lies in their approach to food and diet. Clean eating focuses on consuming whole, unprocessed foods, often labeling certain foods as "clean" or "dirty," and may lead to nutrient deficiencies, food intolerances, and restrictive eating behaviors due to its restrictive nature. Flexible dieting, on the other hand, focuses on tracking macronutrients (protein, carbohydrate, and fat) to achieve a body composition goal, allowing for a greater variety of foods and reducing the likelihood of nutrient deficiencies and food intolerances. Flexible dieting also reduces social restriction and anxiety by allowing for more flexibility in food choices, making it easier to eat out and socialize.

The purpose of the recreational use survey launched by the Surfrider Foundation is to identify spatial information for recreational uses in the Northeast region. The data collected will be used to plan for the health of the ocean and recreational areas, as demands on coastal and marine areas grow. The survey collects information from recreational users about where and how they recreate along New England’s coast, including beach going, wildlife viewing, surfing, and non-motorized boating. The results of the survey will be published in a final report and the spatial data layers will be incorporated into the Northeast Ocean Data Portal to assist the Northeast Regional Planning Body with the ocean planning process. This information will also be combined in the portal with data being collected from recreational industries including commercial whale watching, diving, and marine events. The survey is part of a larger effort by the Surfrider Foundation and its partners to gather data on recreational activities across the coastal United States.

Some tasks that need to be done during spring cleaning for the outdoor area of a house include:

1. Lawns: Clear away any debris, prepare the lawn for its first cut, and review the lawn's condition to decide if it needs to be reduced in size for more low-maintenance features like a larger paved patio area or decking.

2. Patios: Sweep the existing patio with a stiff brush to remove loose detritus, clean it with a warm detergent solution, and rinse carefully with water. Be careful when choosing cleaning agents to avoid using products that contain acid which might adversely affect the appearance of concrete, clay, and natural stone. Inspect the area for any cracked, uneven, or damaged pavers.

3. Ponds: Clear away leaves or twigs, trim back any plants bordering the edge of the pond, and clean or replace the pump and filters. Ensure safety measures are in place for children, such as a secure fence to prevent access to the water.

4. Decking: Clean off the deck with a light pressure wash or stiff brush, and reapply a fresh coating of stain or wood preservative. Consider installing LED lights as a new feature to extend the time spent on the deck and improve the overall ambience of the garden.

5. Fencing: Apply a good quality timber preservative to maintain the condition of the wood. Avoid using traditional creosote or creosote-based preservatives due to health risks. Inspect the perimeter of the garden to ensure all fencing is safe and secure, and consider adjusting the height of the fencing to prevent young children from climbing over it.

6. Garden furniture: Dust off and clean garden furniture if it was not stored during the winter.

7. Tools and equipment: Check the blades of the lawnmower before use, sharpen them if necessary, and clean up any old grass still clinging to the appliance. For petrol-run tools, change the oil annually and check the spark plugs.

Professor Richards, Dr. Smith, and Dr. Paderas used different methods to improve their teaching.

Professor Richards used a checklist method for formative evaluation. She developed a checklist to organize her lectures and revised it after discussing with a colleague who observed her class on three occasions. The colleague provided written comments and ratings after each observation, and they discussed their impressions of the class's organization.

Dr. Smith used a ratings method to increase student involvement during lectures. He asked more questions, left more time for discussion, and included interactive exercises. A colleague completed ratings after each observation, evaluating the instructor's questioning skills, skill in responding to questions, how engaged students were in interactive exercises, and the instructor's skill in facilitating group work.

Dr. Paderas used a narrative log method to understand why she had fallen behind the syllabus in her evening section. She asked a colleague to observe both sections and complete narrative logs, which are records of verbal and nonverbal behaviors but are less intrusive and provide another person's perspective. The colleague provided a detailed account of the lectures in both sections, noting issues such as time management and student attendance.

Additional information about these methods can be found in the Center for Teaching Excellence's guide on Preparing for Peer Observation and in the sourcebook Peer Review of Teaching by N.V.N. Chism.

The dividend capture strategy is a method used by investors to collect dividends without holding the stock for a long period of time. This strategy involves buying a stock before the ex-dividend date and selling it on or after that date. The key dates to understand are the declaration date, ex-dividend date, record date, and pay date.

The dividend capture strategy aims to profit from the stock price movement around the ex-dividend date. If the stock price increases on the ex-dividend date or a few days after, the investor can make capital gains and receive the dividend. However, if the stock price drops past the expected dividend amount, the investor may incur a loss that the dividend may not cover.

There are some things to consider before implementing this strategy, such as taxes, brokerage fees, and market factors. It's important to pay attention to the market trends for a few days prior to purchasing to understand recent trends.

The author of the context has tried the strategy a few times and is working on building a reserve cash supply in their brokerage account for short-term trades like this one.

Some strategies suggested to help alleviate dental anxiety include:

1. Learning relaxation techniques such as yoga, slow and regular breathing, or taking a leisurely walk in nature, and practicing them daily.
2. Speaking to the receptionist about the safety measures they are taking and asking them to inform the dentist about your anxiety. Good dentists will explain each step they take clearly and calmly, and provide a "stop-signal" for you to use if the pain is increasing.
3. Arriving only just before the appointment to avoid extra time to worry.
4. Bringing layers of clothing if it's a cold day, as dentists must open windows to keep air moving, which can make you feel tense.
5. Avoiding excessive caffeine before the appointment, as it can make you feel jittery.
6. Bringing a squeeze ball to control anxiety or listening to calming music on your phone while waiting.
7. Having a companion or friend accompany you or meet you at the surgery and wait outside, as this can help you feel better for the support.

These strategies can help reduce anxiety and make the dental visit more comfortable for you.

The role of civil society organizations, according to the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC), is to provide important research, do valuable field work, and make a significant contribution to policy development in the areas of drug use, crime, and terrorism. UNODC views civil society as an equal partner and needs to be listened to and respected for its expertise. The UNODC works closely with numerous civil society organizations worldwide on a wide range of activities, such as alleviating the suffering of people who use drugs and their families, empowering victims and witnesses of crime, fighting corruption, and establishing effective criminal justice systems. This collaborative work is often carried out in difficult environments, including weak and fragile nations that have recently emerged from conflict and are transitioning towards democracy. Civil society organizations also play a crucial role in the Commission on Narcotic Drugs (CND), where they provide valuable insights and contribute to policy-making. UNODC encourages civil society to continue being the eyes and ears of society and to constructively contribute to the CND.

When Moby-Dick was first published in 1851, it was ridiculed by critics and reviewers. This was largely due to the offense taken by Victorian readers at the novel's impiety and latent atheism. However, it is important to note that Moby-Dick is one of the most aggressively honest explorations of Christianity that emerged from the nineteenth century. The novel's portrayal of God, as a hidden and terrifying power, was a reflection of Melville's own fear and terror of a God who kills and makes alive, a God of wrath, eternal decrees, and unapproachable holiness. Despite the negative reception, there is a sense of grace operating throughout the novel, most obviously in the Epilogue, where Ishmael, the protagonist, survives the sinking of the Pequod. This survival is seen as a gratuitous addition by Melville's reviewers, but it can be interpreted as an act of one-way love by the author towards his creation.

The HAVN ebike-parking unit is a secure and convenient solution for parking and charging electric bikes. It consists of a 55-liter stainless steel rectangular box with two steel brackets: one at the top and another extending from the bottom. The top bracket protects the bike's paint from scratches, while the lower bracket holds the bike's front wheel in place, keeping it upright. Users can store their cycling gear inside the unit's lid and secure their bike using a single lock that goes through both the lid and the main body of the unit.

Additionally, if wired into the local electrical system, the unit has an integrated socket for recharging the ebike's battery. Interested businesses or municipalities can inquire for pricing information via the Street Waves company website. The HAVN ebike-parking unit aims to address the security concerns of expensive e-bikes, which are often targeted by thieves, especially in public places like stores and restaurants.

According to Steve Whyman, the process of creating an artwork involves the following steps:

1. Decide on the subject or subject matter of the artwork.
2. Gather photographs as a basis for the picture. If a photographer's image is used, permission and a fee must be obtained.
3. Use a computer to change and manipulate the image until satisfied with the result.
4. Start with a pencil sketch on a drawing board, usually starting at the top left and working down and to the right. The sketch takes a day or two to produce and includes more and more detail as it progresses.
5. Use Staedtler propelling pencils with various hardness levels and sizes, as well as other types of sketching pencils, erasers, and scalpel blades.
6. For an airbrushed picture, start with a pencil sketch, and spend more time masking and cutting with scalpel blades than actually spraying. Use Iwata airbrushes, Comart, Createx, FW, Magic Colour, Liqutex, and other types of acrylic airbrush paint.
7. Scan the finished artwork at a high resolution and load it into the computer for retouching and tidying up. Add the border, title, copyright info, etc.
8. Do test prints to ensure all colors and details are correct.
9. Print the artwork using large format machines that take rolls of paper or canvas, using 9 colors. The paper and canvas used are of the best quality, and the inks are guaranteed lightfast for 75 years.
10. The prints are limited editions, numbered and signed by Steve Whyman. The gallery frames or varnishes the prints and stretches the canvases using the best materials.
11. In the last few years, Steve Whyman has introduced acrylic and HD metal as new mediums for his artwork, which are produced by specialists in a laboratory-like environment.
12. If a piece of artwork is damaged or the owner wants a different medium, the artwork can be re-printed. If the owner of a sold-out print wants to sell it, Steve Whyman may buy it back.
13. Everyone involved in the production and selling of the artwork will strive to provide the best service possible. If there are any queries, the web site should be contacted.

The Listen Lab evaluates listening effort by measuring pupil dilation, which is considered a reliable index of cognitive load. They are interested in factors affecting listening effort, such as the absolute level of effort and engagement in listening, the speed of understanding sentences, and the amount of time a person needs to spend thinking about a sentence to restore misheard words. The lab also studies how effort is deployed over the course of a sentence and throughout a conversation, how it interacts with language processing, and whether processing parameters of cochlear implants or hearing aids can be changed to reduce effort. They also explore the possibility of using listening effort measures as a tool to capture benefits or weaknesses of listeners for whom absolute percent-correct scores do not tell the whole story.

To find an inexpensive web design company, consider the following tips:

1. Look for single web designers rather than firms, as they often have lower overhead costs and may be willing to work for a more affordable rate.

2. Utilize no-cost advertising methods such as posting flyers at colleges, art schools, and local coffee shops, or using free online platforms like web-based bulletin boards and classified ads.

3. Post a job listing on job boards, but be cautious to avoid those that require payment to list your posting or charge a commission. In your job posting, specify what you want in a designer, request examples of previous work and references, and establish the timeline for delivery of the website along with the budget. Be sure to specify the location of the designer, as working with someone who does not speak English as their first language could lead to complications.

4. Consider using a canned website service if you want the cheapest option and are willing to accept some limitations. These services allow you to choose a design template, customize it, and pay a small fee. To rename the TeamFoundation Server server from tfs.demo.local to tfs1.demo.local, follow these steps:

1. Rename the server: Perform the server renaming process according to your operating system's instructions.

2. Reconfigure the Application Tier using the administration console:
   - Start the admin console.
   - Select the Application Tier node and click the Change URLs link.
   - In the dialog window, update the Notification URL field with the new TFS server address: tfs1.demo.local
   - Click the OK button.

3. If you prefer to use command line tools, you can use the TfsConfig:
   - Open a command prompt.
   - Run the following command to update the notification url in TFS:
   "%programfiles%\Microsoft Team Foundation Server 2010\Tools\tfsconfig" settings /notificationurl:tfs1.demo.local

4. Reconnect the TFS Sharepoint extensions:
   - Start the admin console.
   - Select the Extensions for Sharepoint Products node and click the Modify access link.
   - In the dialog window, update the URL for Team Foundation Server field with the new address: tfs1.demo.local
   - Click OK.

5. Update Office Documents:
   - Open the Product Planning document for editing.
   - In the Team tab, click on Configure and select Server Connection.
   - In the dialog window, click on Servers to add the server using its new URL.
   - When the connection is created, select the server and the correct Team Project.
   - Click on Connect.
   - Repeat steps 2 to 4 for every document.

6. Update the TeamBuild service:
   - On the build server, start the admin console.
   - Select the Build Configuration node and click the Properties link.
   - Update the Connect to Team Project Collection (outgoing) field with the new url: tfs1.demo.local
   - Restart the build service.

7. Update the registry:
   - Open the registry editor (regedit).
   - Go to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\VisualStudio\10.0\TeamFoundation\Build\ServiceHost.
   - Update the ApplicationServerUrl value with the new URL: tfs1.demo.local
   - Restart the build service.

Your installation is back online using the new TFS server name.

The top 5 reasons why Matt Cox, the Chief Revenue Officer, abandons online purchases are:

1. High shipping costs: If the shipping costs are too high, he will look to purchase elsewhere.
2. Poor customer service: He prefers to go with the company or brand that offers the best service and is trusted online, even if the prices are similar.
3. A complicated checkout process: If the checkout process is too long, has a lengthy sign-up, or requires too much time to enter payment details, he will not complete the purchase.
4. Lack of payment options: If there are not several payment options available, he may not be able to complete the purchase due to misplacing his credit card or other payment methods.
5. Just window shopping: He may browse multiple sites looking at items he would like to own but does not have an intention of purchasing at that exact moment. Sending a well-designed, timely remarketing email with the customer's shopping cart details displayed and saved for later can help get some of these hot leads back to the site to complete a purchase.

When performing due diligence on a business you intend to buy, some key factors to consider include:

1. Business certification: Ensure the business has valid permits and licenses to operate, especially in the specific local area where it is located, such as Los Angeles, California.

2. Certificate of good conduct: Investigate the business's reputation and goodwill, as this is crucial for attracting and retaining customers.

3. Zoning laws: Verify that the business complies with local zoning laws, especially for sensitive businesses like nightclubs and bars, which may be restricted from residential areas.

4. Environmental rules and regulations: Check if the business has adhered to local and state environmental regulations to avoid inheriting any potential legal or reputational issues.

5. Leases and other contracts: Review all contracts and legal documents related to the business's premises, equipment, and agreements with customers and vendors to ensure your rights to lease or continue existing agreements.

6. Financial status: Analyze the business's financial records, including balance sheets, sales records, cash flow statements, tax returns, account payables, and advertising costs, to assess its profitability and income stream.

7. Consider multiple options: Perform due diligence on several businesses before making a decision to ensure you have all the necessary information and make an informed decision.

8. Avoid hasty decisions: Take your time to evaluate various options and gather all relevant information to prevent making decisions that could lead to long-term losses and regret.

Based on the provided context, some common scams that people should be aware of during the Christmas season include:

1. Bargain gifts: Scammers may advertise items at unusually low prices to lure people into visiting malware-infected websites.

2. Parcel deliveries: Fake parcel delivery emails can slip through the 'common sense' net during the holiday season. These emails should be deleted without clicking on any links.

3. Bank or PayPal notifications: Scammers may send phishing emails claiming suspicious activity on accounts and asking for security details. These emails should be deleted immediately.

4. Mobile app scams: Novelty or discount shopping apps can contain malware. It's recommended to have a secure Mobile Device Management (MDM) policy in place.

5. E-Cards & novelty websites: These sites can potentially harm company systems and data. Unsolicited emails with links to seasonal frivolity should be deleted immediately.

6. Fake charities: Scammers may send emails pretending to be from charitable organizations. Staff should be vigilant and only support recognized and reputable charities.

7. Romance: Scammers may use dating websites to send phishing scams or infect devices with malware. It's recommended to use only recognized and reputable dating websites.

The 20th anniversary exhibition 'Transition' at CIMA Gallery, Kolkata was significant as it celebrated the gallery's 20 years of exemplary contributions to the art world in India. The exhibition showcased works from a diverse range of artists, both new generation and contemporary pioneers, who have been instrumental in shaping the Indian art scene. The works on display epitomized the genealogy of Indian art at the transitory juncture of local and global influences, forming what is known as 'glocal' art. The exhibition featured works from renowned artists such as Rabindranath Tagore, Ganesh Pyne, Ram Kumar, Manjit Bawa, S.H.Raza, V.S. Gaitonde, Paresh Maity, Samir Aich, Reshm iBagchi Sarkar, Debashis Barui, Sumitro Basak, Tapas Biswas, Jayasri Burman, Sreyashi Chatterjee, Rudranil Das, Sukumar Dutta, Jaya Ganguly, Sushen Ghosh, Somenath Hore, Farhad Hussain, Abir Karmakar, Chittrovanu Mazumdar, Meera Mukherjee, Janak Jhankar Narzary, R.M. Palaniappan, Pankaj Panwar, Lalu Prasad Shaw, Kinkshuk Sarkar, Arpita Singh, Anjum Singh, Paramjit Singh, K.G. Subramanyan, Chintan Upadhyay, Ramesh Tekam, T Vaikuntam, Shilpaguru Ananta Maharana, Jogen Chowdhury, among others. The exhibition was unique in that it represented a historical event, showcasing the embodiment of 20th century Indian art leading into the 21st century Indian art. The works on display were already established and internationally acclaimed, and CIMA Gallery played a significant role in shaping their careers. The exhibition was expected to be re-visited and re-interpreted in the years to come.

The value of an Aboriginal painting is determined by several factors:

1. The Artist: The identity of the artist plays a significant role in determining the value of an Aboriginal painting. Some artists are more renowned and their works are more valuable.

2. Condition: The condition of the painting is another important factor. Damages, wear, and tear can affect the value negatively.

3. Size: The size of the painting also influences its value. Generally, larger paintings are more valuable.

4. Aesthetic Appeal: The overall aesthetic appeal of the painting is a subjective factor that can affect its value. Some paintings may be more visually appealing to collectors, which can increase their value.

5. Known Family History: Any known family history of the piece can also impact its value.

6. Authenticity: Photos of any labels or certificates of authenticity can help establish the authenticity of the painting, which is crucial for its value.

7. Market Demand: The current market demand for Aboriginal art can also affect the value of a painting. Some artists or styles may be more popular at a given time, which can increase the value of their works.

8. Provenance: The provenance, or history of ownership, of the painting can also influence its value. A painting with a known history of ownership by notable collectors or institutions can be more valuable.

9. Sale Method: The method of sale can also affect the value. Selling through a private treaty to a select group of top-end clients may yield a higher price than selling at auction, where the value can be influenced by market fluctuations and the bidding process.

10. Rarity: Paintings by less known artists or rare pieces can also be valuable, especially if they are by early or pioneering artists.

1. Start early: Advertise the internship up to three months before the target start date, and even earlier if possible, to attract a larger number of candidates and more proactive ones.

Some harmful oral habits in children that can lead to tooth decay, misalignments, and difficulty eating or speaking include:

1. Prolonged thumb, finger, or pacifier sucking: This habit can cause open bite of the front teeth, flared upper incisors, tipping of the lower incisors, misalignment of the future permanent teeth, deformities of the roof of the mouth, and other issues.

2. Nail-biting or chewing on objects: This habit can chip away at the enamel, crack the teeth, irritate the soft tissue inside a tooth, and provide an easy entry point for bacteria into the mouth, potentially leading to infections.

3. Playing sports without using a mouthguard: Mouthguards help reduce the chances of severe injuries to the face or mouth during physical activity.

4. Grinding the teeth (bruxism): This habit can cause small cracks in the tooth enamel, wear down the molars, lead to headaches, earaches, and jaw pain, and may be linked to anxiety, stress, and depression.

It's important to address these habits as soon as possible to prevent long-term damage to a child's teeth and oral health. Regular dental and orthodontic check-ups can help identify and address these habits.

The two types of meringue that can be used in the Lemon & Licorice meringue pie recipe are French meringue and Swiss meringue.

French meringue is described as airy and slightly stiff. It is made by beating egg whites until stiff form, then adding sugar 1 tablespoon at a time, whisking constantly until the sugar is completely dissolved. This meringue is decorated on the pie and baked in the oven for 5 minutes.

Swiss meringue, on the other hand, is fluffy, thick, and sticky. It is made by mixing egg whites and sugar in a bowl, then putting the bowl in a water bath and stirring gently until all the sugar is completely dissolved. After that, the site and bottom of the bowl are wiped, and the egg whites are beaten on medium-high speed for 7-10 minutes until the mixture is completely stiff. This meringue is piped on a cold pie and just needs a little burn before serving. It can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 hours before serving.

Both types of meringue are used to top the lemon and licorice pie, but they have different textures and cooking methods.

To transfer ETH from Coinbase to MyEtherWallet, follow these steps:

1. First, ensure you have MyEtherWallet (MEW) set up and have your private key or hardware wallet ready.

2. Log in to your Coinbase account.

3. Navigate to the Accounts section.

4. Select the ETH account you wish to transfer.

5. Click on the "Send" button.

6. Enter the recipient's ETH address (your MyEtherWallet address).

7. Enter the amount of ETH you wish to transfer.

8. Check the network and gas fees, then click "Continue."

9. Confirm the transaction details and click "Send."

10. You will be redirected to a confirmation page. Coinbase will send you an email with the transaction details.

11. Once the transaction is confirmed on the Ethereum network (usually within a few minutes), the ETH will appear in your MyEtherWallet.

The goal of a company, according to the provided context, is to make money by increasing net profit, return on investment, and cash flow. The five steps to achieve this goal when the company's efficiency is not leading to the desired output are:

1. Identify the system's bottleneck.
2. Decide how to exploit the bottleneck (improve the efficiency of the bottleneck, and throughput will increase).
3. Subordinate everything else to the above decision (make sure everything else supports the bottleneck).
4. Elevate the system's bottleneck (provide extra support to reduce the stress on the bottleneck).
5. If a bottleneck has been broken or something else has become the bottleneck, go back to step 1.

These steps are based on the Theory of Constraints, as presented in the book "The Goal: A Process of Ongoing Improvement" by Eliyahu M. Goldratt. The goal of a company, in this context, is not just about efficiency but about increasing throughput, which is the rate at which the system generates money through sales. The focus should be on improving the efficiency of the bottleneck, as the throughput can never be higher than the bottleneck's efficiency.

The document highlights several potential risks associated with extreme fitness training regimens like long-distance running and CrossFit:

1. Lack of Balance: Engaging in extreme fitness training regimens, such as marathons, ultras, and CrossFit games, can lead to neglecting other forms of exercise. This imbalance can result in higher risks of injury.

2. Strength and Flexibility Imbalances: Repeating the same movements and stress patterns on the body can create imbalances, leading to more serious injuries. Some muscles may become too strong while others are ignored, which can require physical therapy and affect normal functioning.

3. Injuries: Higher-intensity, higher-volume training inherently carries a higher risk of injury.

4. Overweight Participants: Distance running should not be used as a weight loss method for overweight individuals, as it can cause more wear on the knees and joints. It's recommended to lose weight through diet first before starting long-distance running.

5. Obsession and Competitiveness: Extreme fitness culture can lead to obsession and competitiveness, pushing individuals beyond their physical capabilities. This can result in injuries and negatively impact other aspects of life, such as job performance and relationships.

6. Unrealistic Goals: Pursuing very high standard goals, such as running 26.2 miles or cleaning 300 pounds, may not contribute to a healthy human life and can consume one's entire life. It's recommended to consider personal goals, why they are important, and adjust training programs accordingly to avoid destructive practices.

To mitigate these risks, the document suggests cross-training, flexibility work, rest, dealing with inflammation and minor injuries properly, and maintaining a well-rounded training program that includes various modalities of fitness.

Antivirus (AV) and Next-Generation Antivirus (NGAV) are security solutions that provide protection against known malicious software (malware) by using signature-based detection. AV is the traditional antivirus software, while NGAV takes it a step further by learning the normal patterns/behaviors of the system to detect anomalous behavior.

Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) is a security solution that focuses on gathering and analyzing security threat-related information from workstations or other endpoints to find indicators of compromise (pre-breach indicators). EDR uses data sources such as log files, performance monitoring sub-systems, file details stored on disk or in memory, running processes, and configuration data. It combines this data with behavioral analysis to protect against emerging threats such as novel malware, exploit chains, ransomware, or advanced persistent threats.

Managed Endpoint Detection and Response (MDR) is an improvement over EDR because it is a managed solution, which means there is an additional layer of intelligence behind the solution usually in the form of a 24/7 human Security Operations Center (SOC). MDR utilizes the information gathered via the EDR system and provides active 24/7 threat monitoring, detection, and some level of remediation service such as guidance or system isolation to contain and prevent the spread of cyber-attacks.

In summary, AV/NGAV provides protection against known threats, EDR focuses on detecting and analyzing potential threats, and MDR offers a managed service that monitors, detects, and responds to threats in real-time.

Processing high-temperature polymers presents several challenges and considerations. These include understanding the special requirements for processing these resins and the limitations of existing extrusion equipment. The extruder and downstream equipment need to be capable of processing at higher temperatures, which may require heating elements that provide long-term serviceability at elevated temperatures. The equipment through which the polymer flows requires more heat to reach the initial startup temperature. High-temperature polymers also require drying at relatively high temperatures. Insulation of the extruder becomes a more critical issue as processing temperatures increase. The surfaces of the extruder line are much hotter than usual, requiring better guarding to prevent inadvertent skin contact. After exiting the die, high-temperature polymers usually require special cooling procedures.

The dividend capture strategy is a method where an investor holds a stock just long enough to receive the dividend. The investor buys a stock before the ex-dividend date and sells it on or sometime after that date. After owning the stock on the ex-dividend date, the investor no longer needs to own the shares to receive the dividend. This strategy can be especially useful when a less attractive company pays a very attractive dividend, allowing the investor to capture the generous dividend without assuming the risk of holding the company over a long period of time.

Steve Whyman starts by deciding what or who he is going to paint and then looks for photographs to serve as the basis for the picture. If he uses a photographer’s image, he asks for permission and pays a fee. He then uses a computer to manipulate the image until he's satisfied with it. He starts with a pencil sketch, which takes about a day to produce. He used to use a super smooth drawing board called CS10, but now uses Schoellershammer 4G from Germany. For a pencil drawing, he starts at the top left and works down and to the right. For an airbrushed picture, he still starts with a pencil sketch, but the starting point depends on the picture and the colours involved. Once the picture is finished, he scans it and loads it into the computer at a high resolution. He then uses a graphics tablet to retouch any blemishes and tidy the image up. He also adds a border around the edge of the image and other details like title, copyright info, etc. He then does some test prints to make sure all the colours and other details are correct. Each print takes about 15 to 20 minutes to pass through the printer. The prints are referred to as “giclée” and are done at KNK Gallery in Nottingham. All his pictures are limited editions and each print is numbered and signed by him.

The two types of meringue that can be used in the Lemon & Licorice meringue pie recipe are French meringue and Swiss meringue. French meringue is airy and slightly stiff, made by beating egg whites until stiff and then adding sugar gradually while whisking until the sugar is completely dissolved. Swiss meringue, on the other hand, is fluffy, thick, and sticky. It is made by mixing egg whites and sugar in a bowl, placing them in a water bath, and stirring gently until all the sugar is completely dissolved. The mixture is then beaten until completely stiff.

Electronics engineering is a field of engineering that deals with the design, development, and application of electrical circuits that involve active electrical components such as vacuum tubes, transistors, diodes, sensors, integrated circuits, and associated passive interconnection technologies. These components are used in information processing, telecommunications, and signal processing. The nonlinear behavior of active components and their ability to control electron flows make digital switching possible. Electronics engineering also includes the study of semiconductors, which are used in most electronic devices today, and the design and construction of electronic circuits to solve practical problems. The underlying principles of how semiconductors work are studied in solid state physics, while the design and construction of electronic circuits is part of electronics engineering.

The Grand Canyon is considered a big deal due to several reasons. Firstly, it is visually overwhelming in size, showcasing a vast and intricate landscape that is both colorful and breathtaking. Secondly, it is one of the six distinct physiographic sections of the Colorado Plateau province. Thirdly, geologically, it is significant because it exposes a thick sequence of ancient rocks that are well preserved and offer a unique opportunity to study much of the early geologic history of the North American continent. Despite the Kali Gandaki Gorge in Nepal being deeper, the Grand Canyon's geological importance and its stunning visual appeal make it a notable natural wonder.

Bodo Tümmler was an athlete who was born in Thorn, West-Prussia (now Toruń, Poland). He was the reigning European Champion in the 1500 meters event. In the 1968 Olympics, he finished in a position behind the Olympic Champion Kipchoge Keino and the world-record holder Jim Ryun. Money is any item or verifiable record that is generally accepted as a medium of exchange for goods and services, repayment of debts, and as a standard of deferred payment within a particular country or socio-economic context. It serves as a unit of account, a store of value, and sometimes, a standard of deferred payment. Historically, money had intrinsic value as a commodity, but most contemporary money systems are based on fiat money without use value, whose value is derived by social convention. The government or regulatory entity declares it as legal tender, meaning it must be accepted as a form of payment within the boundaries of the country. The money supply of a country includes all currency in circulation and bank money, which are the balances held in checking accounts, savings accounts, and other types of bank accounts. Bank money forms the largest part of broad money in developed countries.

The main focus of Mohammad Shah I's reign over his sultanate was the establishment of law and order. He spent his entire reign working towards this goal, ordering his governors not to provide refuge to those who defied his authority. This campaign was successful, resulting in a peaceful and law-abiding land at the end of his reign.

Cabernet Sauvignon is a widely recognized red wine grape variety that originated in France. It is grown in various climates around the world, including regions in Australia, Canada, Lebanon, and many others. The grape is known for its prominence in Bordeaux wines, where it is often blended with Merlot and Cabernet Franc. It has been planted in numerous New World regions such as California, New Zealand, South Africa, Australia, and Chile. For most of the 20th century, it was the world's most widely planted premium red wine grape until it was surpassed by Merlot in the 1990s. However, by 2015, Cabernet Sauvignon had once again become the most widely planted wine grape, with a total of 341,000 hectares (3,410 km2) under vine worldwide.

A byte is a unit of digital information that most commonly consists of eight bits. It is the smallest addressable unit of memory in many computer architectures and was historically used to encode a single character of text in a computer. In network protocol documents, an 8-bit byte is referred to as an octet. The bits in an octet are usually numbered from 0 to 7 or 7 to 0, with the first bit being number 0.

Henri-Cartier Bresson is known for his photography, particularly for his concept of "The Decisive Moment." This concept refers to the ability to capture a fleeting, significant moment in time through a photograph. He is also recognized for his book "The Decisive Moment," which was first published in French as "Images à la sauvette" in 1952. This book includes a collection of his photographs from various parts of the world.

In the provided context, electricity is not recorded in ancient Egyptian texts in any specific way as the concept of electricity as we understand it today did not exist during that time. However, they were aware of electric shocks from electric fish, which they referred to as the "Thunderer of the Nile" and described as the "protectors" of all other fish. They also knew that certain objects, such as rods of amber, could be rubbed with cat's fur to attract light objects like feathers, but they did not understand the scientific principle behind this phenomenon. The term "electricity" was first coined by William Gilbert in 1600, and the study of electricity as a scientific field began to develop in the 17th and 18th centuries.

In postcolonial theory, the term "inversion" refers to a form of resistance or retaliation by the subordinate groups against the dominant colonizers. This concept was first introduced by the Subaltern Studies school, particularly by Ranajit Guha, who discussed it as a modality of peasant revolt in colonial India. Inversion involves a reversal of the colonizer's monopoly on violence, aiming to break out of the master-slave dialectic, cultural inferiority complex, and learned helplessness of the colonized.

The term "inversion" is also used to theorize violence, especially when actions against members of structurally dominant groups are taken by structurally subordinate groups. Examples of such actions include Palestinian suicide bombing, the 9/11 attacks, land reform in Zimbabwe, the writings and actions of Valerie Solanas and SCUM, and what are treated by the state as racially motivated crimes against white people.

Moreover, attaching positive values to an essence of the oppressed, as in some black-consciousness and Afrocentric ideas, would also be an instance of inversion, especially to critics. However, Edward Said argues against this inversion, suggesting that such essences have much to do with the native manipulators and the embattled imperial contexts out of which they came.

OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) is a type of information system that facilitates and manages transaction-oriented applications. These applications are designed to handle a high volume of transactions, such as those in the realm of business or finance, and are insert- or update-intensive in database management. OLTP systems are used concurrently by hundreds of users and prioritize goals like availability, speed, concurrency, and recoverability (durability). An example of a commercial transaction processing application is an automated teller machine (ATM) for a bank. OLTP is often contrasted with OLAP (online analytical processing) and OLEP (online event processing).

Romania's history began with settlements in the Lower Paleolithic era. The kingdom of Dacia existed, which was later conquered and Romanized by the Roman Empire during late antiquity. The modern Romanian state was formed in 1859 through a union of the Danubian Principalities of Moldavia and Wallachia, named Romania since 1866. Romania gained independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1877. During World War I, Romania initially declared neutrality in 1914 but later joined the Allied Powers in 1916. After the war, it gained territories including Bukovina, Bessarabia, Transylvania, and parts of Banat, Crișana, and Maramureș. However, in 1940, due to the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact and Second Vienna Award, Romania was compelled to cede Bessarabia and Northern Bukovina to the Soviet Union and Northern Transylvania to Hungary. Romania then joined the Axis Powers in World War II, fighting against the Soviet Union until 1944, when it switched sides and joined the Allies, recovering Northern Transylvania. After the war, Romania became a socialist republic and a member of the Warsaw Pact. Following the 1989 Revolution, Romania transitioned towards democracy and a market economy.
There are two disciplines of rowing: sculling and sweep rowing. In sculling, each rower holds two oars, one in each hand, while in sweep rowing each rower holds one oar with both hands.

Ahilya Bai was the one who took over the affairs of the Holkar dynasty after the demise of her husband Khande Rao Holkar and father-in-law Malhar Rao Holkar. She was a military leader who defended the Malwa state against intruders and personally led armies into battle, with Tukoji Rao Holkar serving as her military commander.

Ahilya Bai is also known for her significant contributions to the construction of Hindu temples and Dharmashalas (rest houses for travelers) throughout India. She is particularly renowned for refurbishing and reconsecrating some of the most sacred Hindu pilgrimage sites that were desecrated and demolished by the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in the previous century.

The United States Bill of Rights is the first ten amendments to the United States Constitution. It comprises specific guarantees of personal freedoms and rights, clear limitations on the government's power in judicial and other proceedings, and explicit declarations that all powers not specifically granted to the federal government by the Constitution are reserved to the states or the people. The Bill of Rights was proposed following the debate over the ratification of the Constitution, and it was largely due to the efforts of Representative James Madison. The Bill of Rights was ratified on December 15, 1791, and became Amendments One through Ten of the Constitution. It applies only to the federal government, but since the early 20th century, both federal and state courts have used the Fourteenth Amendment to apply portions of the Bill of Rights to state and local governments. There are several original engrossed copies of the Bill of Rights still in existence, one of which is on permanent public display at the National Archives in Washington, D.C.

Postmodernism is an intellectual stance or mode of discourse that emerged in the mid-twentieth century, initially from literary criticism. It is characterized by skepticism towards the "grand narratives" of modernism, rejection of epistemic certainty, and sensitivity to the role of ideology in maintaining political power. Postmodernism questions claims to objectivity, emphasizing the conditional nature of knowledge claims within particular historical, political, and cultural discourses.

Key features of postmodernism include self-referentiality, epistemological relativism, moral relativism, pluralism, irony, irreverence, and eclecticism. It rejects the "universal validity" of binary oppositions, stable identity, hierarchy, and categorization. Postmodernism is associated with the disciplines of deconstruction and post-structuralism. Some critics argue that postmodernism promotes obscurantism, abandons Enlightenment rationalism and scientific rigor, and adds nothing to analytical or empirical knowledge.

"The Sun Also Rises" is a novel by Ernest Hemingway, published in 1926. The MECE principle, short for Mutually Exclusive and Collectively Exhaustive, is a grouping strategy that separates a set of items into subsets that are mutually exclusive (ME) and collectively exhaustive (CE). This principle ensures that each item belongs to only one subset and that all items are accounted for in the grouping.

In simpler terms, the MECE principle means that the categories are disjoint (mutually exclusive) and cover all possible cases (collectively exhaustive). For example, if we categorize books by their genre (fiction, non-fiction, biography, etc.), each book belongs to only one category, and all books are accounted for in the categorization, making it a MECE arrangement.

In business, the MECE principle can be used to structure problem-solving, decision-making, and analysis processes. By organizing information in a MECE manner, analysts can ensure that they have considered all relevant factors and have not overlooked any important aspects. For instance, when analyzing a market, a company might use the MECE principle to categorize customers by demographics (age, income, location, etc.) and by purchasing behavior (frequency, product preferences, etc.). This approach helps the company to understand the market better, identify trends, and develop targeted strategies.

The Old English Bible translations were created primarily to assist clerics whose grasp of Latin was imperfect. These translations were made from Latin texts, as the original languages of the Bible were not yet available in written form at that time. The translations were often in the form of Bible glosses, which were used to help with understanding the Vulgate Latin Bible, the standard Bible in Western Christianity during the medieval period. The Old English language was one of the few early medieval vernacular languages in which the Bible was translated, and these translations included incomplete versions like the Paris Psalter and Ælfric's Hexateuch, which were intended for circulation.

Team effectiveness, as described in the context, is the ability of a team to accomplish the goals or objectives assigned by an authorized personnel or the organization. This capacity is demonstrated when a group of individuals, who are interdependent in their tasks, share responsibility for outcomes, and perceive themselves as a unified entity within an institutional or organizational system, successfully meet their established objectives. The team's effectiveness is often evaluated through factors such as group cohesiveness and teamwork, which are crucial for the team's success. It's important to note that while teams and groups share similarities in terms of processes and research related to their effectiveness, they are distinct entities. Groups and their members are independent of each other's role, skill, knowledge, or purpose, whereas teams and their members are interdependent upon each other's role, skill, knowledge, and purpose.

In Machine Learning, unsupervised learning is a type of algorithm that learns patterns from untagged data, meaning the data does not have predefined labels or categories. The machine is forced to build a concise representation of its world and then generate imaginative content from it. This is different from supervised learning, where data is tagged or labeled by an expert, such as a "ball" or "fish". In unsupervised learning, the machine exhibits self-organization that captures patterns as probability densities or a combination of neural feature preferences encoded in the machine's weights and activations. The other levels in the supervision spectrum are reinforcement learning, where the machine is given only a numerical performance score as guidance, and semi-supervised learning, where a small portion of the data is tagged.

Binary search is a search algorithm used in computer science to find the position of a target value within a sorted array. It works by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half. If the target value is not equal to the middle element of the array, the search continues on the half where the target cannot lie. This process repeats until the target value is found or the remaining half is empty, indicating that the target is not in the array. The time complexity of binary search is O(log n), where n is the number of elements in the array. However, the array must be sorted first to apply binary search. There are variations of binary search, such as fractional cascading and exponential search, and data structures like binary search tree and B-tree that are based on binary search.

A data warehouse is a system used for reporting and data analysis, which is a crucial component of business intelligence. It serves as a central repository for integrated data from various sources, storing both current and historical data in one single location. This centralization allows companies to create analytical reports for employees across the enterprise, enabling them to interrogate and draw insights from their data, make informed decisions, and improve overall business performance.

The data in a data warehouse is sourced from operational systems such as marketing or sales. Before being used for reporting, the data may pass through an operational data store and undergo data cleansing to ensure its quality. There are two main approaches to building a data warehouse system: Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) and Extract, Load, Transform (ELT). In ETL, data is extracted from the source systems, transformed to fit the data warehouse schema, and then loaded into the data warehouse. In ELT, data is first loaded into the data warehouse, and then transformed. Both approaches aim to prepare the data for efficient analysis and reporting.

The sources of natural hydrogen, as mentioned in the provided context, are:

1. Degassing of deep hydrogen from the Earth's crust and mantle.
2. Reaction of water with ultrabasic rocks (serpentinisation).
3. Contact of water with reducing agents in the Earth's mantle.
4. Interaction of water with freshly exposed rock surfaces (weathering).
5. Decomposition of hydroxyl ions in the structure of minerals.
6. Natural radiolysis of water.
7. Decomposition of organic matter.
8. Biological activity.
9. Diagenetic origin (iron oxidation) in the sedimentary basins of cratons, notably in Russia.
10. Mantle hydrogen.
11. Hydrogen from radiolysis (natural electrolysis).
12. Hydrogen from bacterial activity.

The context also mentions that natural hydrogen is extracted from wells, often mixed with other gases such as nitrogen or helium. Several sources have been identified in France, and large reservoirs have been found in a dozen countries, including Mali and the United States. Numerous emanations on the ocean floor have been identified but are difficult to exploit. The discovery of a significant emergence in Russia in 2008 suggests the possibility of extracting native hydrogen in geological environments.

Captain Oliver Cromwell Applegate was an American politician, newspaper editor, and Indian agent in Oregon. He was born on June 11, 1845, in Yamhill District, Oregon, and was the sixth son and seventh child of Lindsay Applegate, a prominent pioneer and explorer. Applegate attended school in Ashland, Oregon, and served as a teacher for four years. In 1863, he became a captain of the "Mountain Rangers," a cavalry company in Southern Oregon.

In 1865, Applegate worked as an assistant to his father, who was the United States Indian Agent over the Klamaths and Modocs at Fort Klamath. He played a significant role in the Modoc War, serving as a scout and using his influence over the tribes to help resolve the conflict. In 1873, he became a U.S. Commissioner with jurisdiction committed against federal law locally.

In 1876, Applegate was appointed general Indian agent for Oregon, a position he held for several years. During this time, he helped establish Indian claims to over half a million dollars for lands excluded from the reservation by erroneous boundary surveys and developed comprehensive plans for irrigation and drainage. In 1898, he took charge of the Klamath Reservation as United States Indian agent, and served as such for five years.

Applegate served as editor of the Ashland Tidings starting in 1878, and later edited the Klamath Republican. He was married to Ella Anderson in 1878 and had three sons and three daughters. Applegate died on October 11, 1938, at the age of 93. He is the namesake of Applegate Peak in Crater Lake National Park.

A typical advantage of single-cell multiomics versus bulk analysis is that it allows for the mitigation of confounding factors derived from cell-to-cell variation. This advantage enables the uncovering of heterogeneous tissue architectures, providing an unprecedented resolution to study multilevel transitions in health and disease at the single-cell level. Single-cell multiomics offers insights that cannot be gathered solely from transcriptomic analysis, as it includes information regarding non-coding genomic regions and copy-number variation, among other details.

A dispersive prism is an optical prism that is used to separate light into its spectral components, or the colors of the rainbow. This is achieved because the prism material's index of refraction varies with wavelength, causing different wavelengths (colors) of light to be deflected at different angles. Longer wavelengths (red) undergo a smaller deviation than shorter wavelengths (blue). The dispersion of white light into colors by a prism was a key observation by Sir Isaac Newton that led him to conclude that white light consists of a mixture of different colors.

Manchester City Football Club, often referred to as Man City, is an English professional football club based in Manchester. Established in 1880 as St. Mark's (West Gorton), it was later named Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. The team plays in the Premier League, the highest tier of English football, and their home ground is the Etihad Stadium in east Manchester, which they moved to in 2003 after playing at Maine Road since 1923.

Manchester City's home kit is sky blue, a color they adopted in 1894, their first season with the current name. The club has won a total of eight league titles, six FA Cups, eight League Cups, six FA Community Shields, and one European Cup Winners' Cup. They joined the Football League in 1892 and won their first major trophy, the FA Cup, in 1904.

The club experienced a significant period of success in the late 1960s and early 1970s under the management of Joe Mercer and Malcolm Allison, winning the league title, FA Cup, League Cup, and European Cup Winners Cup. However, after losing the 1981 FA Cup Final, the club went through a period of decline, culminating in relegation to the third tier of English football for the only time in their history in 1998. Following promotion to the top tier in 2001–02, they have remained in the Premier League since 2002–03.

The Parthian Empire, also known as the Arsacid Empire, and the Arsacid Empire are one and the same. The name Arsacid comes from its founder, Arsaces I, who led the Parni tribe in conquering the region of Parthia. The Parthian Empire was a major Iranian political and cultural power that existed from 247 BC to 224 AD. Mithridates I (ruled around 171-132 BC) expanded the empire by seizing Media and Mesopotamia from the Seleucids. At its height, the Parthian Empire stretched from the northern reaches of the Euphrates, in what is now central-eastern Turkey, to present-day Afghanistan and western Pakistan. The Parthian Empire was located on the Silk Road trade route between the Roman Empire and the Han dynasty of China, becoming a center of trade and commerce.

The term "Parthian Empire" and "Arsacid Empire" are interchangeable and refer to the same historical entity. There is no significant difference between the two names.

The main characteristics of a Haiku poem, as traditionally defined, are as follows:

1. It consists of three phrases.
2. The number of phonetic units (on) in each line is 5, 7, and 5 respectively.
3. It includes a kireji, or "cutting word."
4. It includes a kigo, or seasonal reference.
5. It originated as an opening part of a larger Japanese poem called renga.
6. Haiku can be written as stand-alone poems.
7. Haiku written in English and other languages may vary in how closely they follow these traditional elements.
8. In Japanese, haiku are traditionally printed as a single line, while in English, they often appear as three lines.
9. There are related forms of Japanese poetry such as tanka, and art forms that incorporate haiku, such as haibun and haiga.

The main benefit of the fianchetto in chess openings is that it often allows the fianchettoed bishop to become more active. This increased activity can help in controlling key diagonal lines and exerting pressure on the opponent's position. However, it's important to note that if the fianchettoed bishop is exchanged, the squares it was protecting may become weak, potentially creating opportunities for the opponent to launch an attack. Therefore, exchanging the fianchettoed bishop should not be done lightly, especially if the enemy bishop on the same-colored squares is still on the board.

To win in Texas Hold 'em, a player needs to have the best five-card poker hand using any combination of their two hole cards and the five community cards. The rounds of betting take place before the flop is dealt and after each subsequent deal. The player who has the best hand and has not folded by the end of all betting rounds wins all of the money bet for the hand, known as the pot. In certain situations, a split-pot or tie can occur when two players have hands of equivalent value. This is also called a "chop-pot".

The Sukhoi-Gulfstream S-21 was a joint project between Gulfstream Aerospace and the Sukhoi Design Bureau, initiated in the early 1990s, aimed at developing a supersonic small business jet. The goal was to create an aircraft capable of sustained cruise at Mach 2+.

However, due to questionable market demand for commercial supersonic air travel, the commitment to the project weakened, and delays mounted. Gulfstream eventually dissolved the partnership, although Sukhoi continued work on the S-21.

The project involved significant research and development to manage the troublesome transonic effects phenomena associated with near Mach 1 air speeds. As of 2012, the project had not received any funding for the 2013-2025 development period and appeared to be cancelled.

In Christianity, salvation refers to the process by which human beings are saved from sin and its consequences, which include death and separation from God. This salvation is achieved through Christ's death and resurrection, and the justification that follows. The faithful are believed to be redeemed by participating in Jesus' death and rising. Early Christians saw themselves as partaking in a new covenant with God, open to both Jews and Gentiles, through the sacrificial death and subsequent exaltation of Jesus Christ. There are various theories about the person and sacrificial role of Jesus in human salvation, such as the ransom theory, Christus Victor theory, recapitulation theory, satisfaction theory, penal substitution theory, and moral influence theory. Different Christian denominations may have variant views on salvation, including conflicting definitions of sin, depravity, justification, and atonement.

The main functions on a DSLR camera that affect the correct exposure of an image are shutter speed and f-number (aperture). The document explains that exposure value (EV) is a number that represents a combination of these two factors, such that all combinations that yield the same exposure have the same EV. Changing either the shutter speed or the f-number will change the EV and thus the exposure of the image. The document also mentions that some DSLR cameras have features that link the shutter and aperture controls to maintain the same exposure when one is changed, which can be helpful for both beginners and experienced photographers.

StarCraft is a real-time strategy video game developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment. It was first released in 1998 for Microsoft Windows, with subsequent versions for Mac OS and Nintendo 64 following in 1999 and 2000, respectively. The game is set in the Koprulu Sector of the Milky Way galaxy during the 25th century AD, where three intelligent species - the Terrans (humans), Zerg (insectoid aliens), and Protoss (humanoid aliens with advanced technology and psionic abilities) - are fighting for dominance.

StarCraft is considered one of the most important and greatest video games of all time by many journalists in the video game industry. It is known for its unique factions in RTS gameplay and compelling storyline. The game has sold over 11 million copies worldwide by February 2009, making it one of the best-selling games for the personal computer.

The storyline of StarCraft has been expanded through a series of novels, the expansion pack StarCraft: Brood War, and two officially authorized add-ons, Insurrection and Retribution. A sequel, StarCraft II: Wings of Liberty, was released in July 2010, with two expansion packs and a campaign pack following between 2013 and 2016. A remastered edition of the original game and its expansion pack was released in August 2017, and the original game was made available for free in April 2017.

Sim racing, as stated in the context, refers to simulated racing or racing simulation. It is a type of racing game software that aims to replicate real-world auto racing, including factors such as fuel usage, damage, tire wear, and suspension settings. To excel in sim racing, a driver needs to understand various aspects of car handling, including threshold braking, maintaining control during loss of traction, and proper entry and exit of turns without compromising speed. This level of difficulty sets sim racing apart from arcade racing-style games that prioritize speed over realism.

Sim racing requires faster computers to run effectively and a racing wheel and pedals for a more immersive experience. While gamepads, joysticks, or even a mouse and keyboard can be used for arcade-style driving games, they do not provide the same level of immersion and realism as a racing wheel and pedals. In recent years, sim racing experiences have been developed for consoles like PlayStation and Xbox, although they can still be played with a controller. However, it is recommended to invest in a racing wheel and pedals for a better experience. Online racing allows players to compete against human opponents and computer AI offline, offering an experience that is closest to driving cars on a real track for many. Even professional racers use simulations for practice or entertainment. With the advancement of physics engine software and improved hardware, the sim racing experience has become increasingly realistic.

Examples of sim racing video games include iRacing, Assetto Corsa, Assetto Corsa Competizione, Gran Turismo, and others.

The Spanish Civil War took place from 1936 to 1939, starting with a coup d'état by a part of the military on July 17 and 18. The country was divided into two zones: the Republican government-controlled zone, which received support from the Soviet Union, Mexico, and International Brigades, and the Nationalist or rebel-controlled zone, backed by Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy. The Western powers did not support the Republican government due to a British-led policy of non-intervention. General Francisco Franco was sworn in as the supreme leader of the rebels on October 1, 1936.

The war was characterized by numerous atrocities committed by all sides, resulting in over 500,000 deaths and the displacement of up to half a million citizens. The Republican government and grassroots anarchists also had an uneasy relationship. The Nationalist side, led by Franco, emerged victorious on April 1, 1939, imposing a dictatorship over the entire country. Thousands of prisoners were held in Francoist concentration camps after the war, with an estimated 367,000 to 500,000 prisoners in 50 camps or prisons.

Gabon is a relatively rich country compared to most nations in sub-Saharan Africa due to its abundance of natural resources, particularly oil, manganese, and timber. The country is the fifth largest oil producer in Africa, and the oil sector accounts for 50% of the country's GDP and 80% of its exports. This has helped drive Gabon's strong growth in the later 20th century, and has contributed to a per capita income that is four times higher than that of most nations in the region. However, oil production has been declining since its peak in 1997, and periods of low oil prices have had a negative impact on government revenues and the economy. The government has plans to diversify its economy away from its dwindling oil reserves, and is considering the development of iron ore reserves as a potential source of future growth.

In the Roman Catholic Church, a consistory refers to a formal meeting of the College of Cardinals, called by the pope. There are two types of consistories: extraordinary and ordinary. An "extraordinary" consistory is a meeting where the pope consults with the entire membership of the College of Cardinals. On the other hand, an "ordinary" consistory is a ceremonial meeting attended by cardinals residing in Rome. Notably, the pope elevates new cardinals to the College during a consistory, and Pope Francis has called consistories for ceremonies of canonization.

The planet Jupiter is primarily made up of hydrogen and helium. In its upper atmosphere, hydrogen constitutes about 90% by volume, and helium about 10%. By mass, Jupiter's atmosphere is approximately 24% helium. The atmosphere also contains trace amounts of methane, water vapor, ammonia, and silicon-based compounds, as well as fractional amounts of carbon, ethane, hydrogen sulfide, neon, oxygen, phosphine, and sulfur. The outermost layer of the atmosphere contains crystals of frozen ammonia. The interior of Jupiter, however, contains denser materials, with hydrogen making up about 71% by mass, helium about 24%, and the remaining 5% consisting of various other elements.

A Lie algebra is a vector space equipped with a bilinear map called the Lie bracket, which satisfies the Jacobi identity. This structure allows for the study of infinitesimal transformations of a Lie group. In simpler terms, a Lie algebra is an algebraic structure that describes the structure of the infinitesimal generators of a Lie group.

The Lie bracket operation [.,.] between elements X and Y in the Lie algebra is defined as the derivative at the identity element of the commutator of the corresponding Lie group elements. The Lie bracket satisfies the following properties:

1. Bilinearity: [aX + bY, Z] = a[X, Z] + b[Y, Z] and [Z, aX + bY] = a[Z, X] + b[Z, Y] for all scalars a and b.
2. Antisymmetry: [X, Y] = -[Y, X].
3. Jacobi identity: [X, [Y, Z]] + [Y, [Z, X]] + [Z, [X, Y]] = 0.

The Lie algebra of a Lie group G is the space of left-invariant vector fields on G. The Lie bracket of two left-invariant vector fields is again a left-invariant vector field, making the space of left-invariant vector fields a Lie algebra.

The classification of simple Lie algebras was first achieved by Wilhelm Killing and later perfected by Élie Cartan. This classification is known as the Killing-Cartan classification. Simple Lie algebras, along with the Lie algebra of the real numbers and the Lie algebra of the unit-magnitude complex numbers, serve as the building blocks for all finite-dimensional connected Lie algebras. Many commonly encountered Lie algebras are either simple or very close to being simple, such as the special linear Lie algebra SL(n) for n > 1.
